text:
There once was a sleepy squirrel named Rusty. Every morning, Rusty would wake up with the sun shining through the trees. He'd scamper down the oak branches, fill his cheeks with nuts, and prepare his day. One crisp morning, as Rusty was munching on his breakfast berries, he heard a peculiar sound. It wasn't the cheerful chirping of birds. No, it was a voice.

"I could really go for a snack."

Rusty looked around. Nothing like that in the oak tree. Then, his bushy tail twitched—it wasn't an owl!

"A snack? From me?"

He thought to himself. A voice like that could only mean... Chocolate?

Before Rusty could even ponder chocolate bars, he noticed something unusual. A little mouse was hopping closer.

"There you are—" said the mouse.

"I'll take you for a snack?"

"N-No! I've got lots of things I'd like. Why chocolate?"

"It tastes excellent!"

Hmm. Not
